issues.title: Please add a config of mcp server request timeout, default 10s is too short.
issues.body:
Please add a config to modify the  request time of mcp server in the McpServerAutoConfiguration.java .
The mcp server has a default value of 10s,  it's too short.


SyncSpecification serverBuilder = McpServer.sync(transportProvider).serverInfo(serverInfo);
serverBuilder.requestTimeout(Duration.of(xxxxx))

issues.title: Cleanup accessing environment variables for api keys using System.getEnv in reference documentation.
issues.body:
We should not use System envrionment variables that are the same as the Spring ai application properties as this maybe confusing to the end users. This gives the implication that they should always set the enviroment variables matching the Spring ai application properties, which is not the case.

For e.g, the following should be replaced by the applicaton.properties ( `spring.ai.openai.api-key` for example)

export SPRING_AI_OPENAI_API_KEY=<INSERT GROQ API KEY HERE>
export SPRING_AI_OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://api.groq.com/openai
export SPRING_AI_OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL=llama3-70b-8192

If you want to use api keys as environment vars, then you can use any env vars for that and then retireve that using SpEL or programmatically (using System.getEnv(..))

We will close this PR: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3072

and then clean up the docs wherever we use env vars.

issues.title: CassandraVectorStore.checkSchemaValid does not check if `this.schema.index()` exists
issues.body:
**current behavior**

If we set an **invalid** index name when setting up `CassandraVectorStore` using it's builder
```
CassandraVectorStore.Builder builder = CassandraVectorStore.builder(...).indexName("some_invalid_index_name")
```

Inside `CassandraVectorStore`, there is the `ensureSchemaExists` method, which calls `checkSchemaValid` when `this.initializeSchema` is `false`.
Inside `checkSchemaValid`, it does not check if the `indexName` exists, and then inside `getIndexSimilarity`, it tries to get the index from `metadata` like this (Line 398):
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/3fb66928f5e1434b5a67eb150931f21bdf601079/vector-stores/spring-ai-cassandra-store/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/vectorstore/cassandra/CassandraVectorStore.java#L398
```
private Similarity getIndexSimilarity(TableMetadata metadata) {

		return Similarity.valueOf(metadata.getIndex(this.schema.index())
			.get()
			.getOptions()
			.getOrDefault("similarity_function", "COSINE")
			.toUpperCase());
	}
```
and then calls `.get()` on the optional, which will throw a vague exception `java.util.NoSuchElementException: No value present`.

**expected behavior**
In my opinion, because `checkSchemaValid` is already validating keyspace name, table name, and column names, I think it should also explicitly check the index name too, to avoid the exception.
